description.en-US: A late treatise on the angels (the *substantiae separatae*) — their existence, nature, hierarchy, and operations. Aquinas's first survey of pagan-philosophical and patristic teaching on the angels before establishing his own position.
description.la: Tractatus serotinus de angelis (*substantiae separatae*) — eorum existentia, natura, hierarchia, operationibus. Doctrinae paganae-philosophicae et patristicae de angelis recensio prima Thomae antequam suam positionem stabiliret.

sources.description: English translation by Francis J. Lescoe (West Hartford, 1959), revised by the Aquinas Institute. Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Prologue — Prologue
Chapter 1 — On the opinions of the early philosophers and of Plato
Chapter 2 — On the opinion of Aristotle
Chapter 3 — How the positions of Aristotle and Plato agree
Chapter 4 — How the positions of Aristotle and Plato differ
Chapter 5 — On the opinion of Avicebron and his arguments for it
Chapter 6 — Refutation of Avicebron’s position
Chapter 7 — That of spiritual and corporeal substances there cannot be one matter
Chapter 8 — Refutation of Avicebron’s arguments
Chapter 9 — On the opinion of those who say that spiritual substances are not created
Chapter 10 — Against those who posit that not all spiritual substances are immediately from God
Chapter 11 — Against the Platonists, who posit that certain essential perfections of spiritual substances are not immediately from God
Chapter 12 — Against Origen, who posited that all spiritual substances are created equal
Chapter 13 — On the error of some concerning the knowledge and providence of spiritual substances
Chapter 14 — In which it is shown that God has knowledge of everything
Chapter 15 — That the care of divine providence extends to all things
Chapter 16 — Resolution of the aforementioned positions
Chapter 17 — On the error of the Manicheans concerning spiritual substances
Chapter 18 — On the origin of spiritual substances according to the Catholic faith
Chapter 19 — On the condition of spiritual substances
Chapter 20 — On the distinction of angelic spirits
